channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Everest MiCA CASP — 93 replies — 238.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Everest MiCA CASP

Open thread • Replies: 93 • Updated: 2026-07-16 19:07 UTC

OP

I want to personally thank every Everest ID hater in here, talking to you Link baggies. They secured MiCA CASP last week.

A single MFSA license is valid across 27 EU states, reaching ~450M people. Of the ~283 CASPs on the ESMA register, nearly all are exchanges or brokers. Everest is one of the rare licensed infrastructure providers: identity and payment rails, not another venue.

Enjoy it furries

Early replies (first 3)

62461998
>>62461988
I thought it would have mooned harder from the news. What gives

62462006
It is because we are meant to suffer as token holders just like the Linkies

62463131
>Unlisted from coingecko
Lmao!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62482133
This is where the chads ape, shitcoin down 99.8%, MiCA CASP just issued, so they do not have to worry about AI taking their job and being on Elon’s high universal income AKA you do everything we say or we blacklist you. Peter Thiel as well you will have to obey him too if no ID bag. It begins.

62482516
>fat tranny still rambling about holding a scam coin

lol

lmao

62482527
This is regarding Bitboy marketing Everest 3 years ago right? If so, rest assured, no more fat trannies on the team as of past few years innit

62482743
member when they onboarded some emerald mine and gave them millions in free tokens which they dumped on holders i member

62483435
>>62482743
this cant be real lmao

62483487
It’s a lie lol, mining company bought 1 million ID at 25 cents via otc to use Everest for their RWA. That 1 million ID was never sold on market….retards

62484212
BUMP! That was a close one! Cmon eversisters, we have to do better at keeping this thread alive

62484271
The price keeps rising holy shit

62484289
Don’t fall for this transphobic scam. Ever since my MtF surgery Bob refuses to update my biometrics and now I have no access to tokens.

62484582
>>62484271
He’ll yea, brother! We went from $1.22m to $1.28m! Nocoiners on suicide watch! Watch them cope, seethe, and dial the number 8! Now check my freaking digits!

62485043
>>62484582
Check now :) just getting started too

62485430
>>62485043
>$1.2m to $1.4m
>smugface.jpeg
Heck freaking yea. We are sooo totally gonna make it! I can hear the haters dial8’ing as we speak ;^)

62485733
Haters better get their rope ready

62485738
May the 4 Chan mods please pin this super important thread?

62485915
>>62485733
That’s right, BJ Bob. I just bought 150 shares of CopeRope™ because the HATERS are so totally going to rope themselves haha

62486225
I’ve tried to rope the last 5 years to save myself the pain from holding this dog shit token but here we are today kek

62486253
>>62486225
Hey eversister, you forgot to change your ID. Please delete before others see this

62486286
Holy retard yes I’m the original poster. I’m not a feg like some of you 4channers with 10 alts to boost your little egos, grow up tardos. Bob will shit in your faces and my years of mental disease will magically disappear

62487330
Just commenting during my 3 am middle of night parasite dump, ID still exists

62487641
>>62487330
Thanks for saving the thread. I was asleep and didn’t wake up in time to bump it lol

62487651
fake buys going on rn

62487724
Whats the sui / make it stack?

62487799
The forEverest schizophrenics lighting their USD and Euro on fire the last 5 years would say 1m ID sui

62487913
10m ID, we need a few whales, I’m talking absolute mammoth booty sitting on my face until I die from asphyxiation type of whale

62487917
>>62487799
>>62487913
??
I am not buying 12k or 120k of this 1.4mcap shitcoin

62487944
Go look at marketcap of other tokens with MiCA CASP that Binance could not get lol

62488146
>>62487917
100k sui 400k make it

62488344
We need one of the holders to go through a humiliation ritual, I think that’s when it will start running and never return to earth again

62489235
>>62470842
good. fuck that guy
can't believe he turned his show into an Everest shill

62489843
Bob said he is a mix of Kobe and Tiger Woods the other week. I don’t know much about sports but I think those are very good players who won championships!

item.title: AirFox — 40 replies — 160.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
AirFox

Open thread • Replies: 40 • Updated: 2026-07-16 19:27 UTC

OP

Any qrd on what’s going on with this thing? What exactly are these guys hoping to accomplish? Crypto is cooked and they keep trying to change this project in ways that don’t seem to matter. The TG link also doesn’t work anymore.

Early replies (first 3)

62481067
>>62480640
>2 hours later
>0 replies
jfc it’s worse than I thought. These threads used to reach bump limits after a couple hours.

62481088
>>62480640
it's a dead shitcoin, the devs are just waiting for the few final bagholders to give up so they can wrap it up and enjoy the money they made selling this shit

62481280
>>62480640
its just a rebrand and a relaunch with shifty eyes about robinhood instead of base.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62481704
>>62481552
I got in just after launch with 60m and halved that since then. All signs are pointing to this project dying off. There is no other way around it desu. 2023 and early 2024 were comfy. The team could have done stuff differently back then to get this shilled better outside of biz but for the most part, crypto is fucking dead. That’s out of their control. It would be better if they can come up with a way to just sunset the project and finish it off. Sell everything off and distribute the eth to everyone who held on all these years rather than wallets slowly draining away whatever is left of the liquidity.

62482029
WTFWT HAHAHAHAHA STIXIL YA MADLAD I KNEW YOU COULD DO IT!!!! I NEVER DOUBTED YOU WAGMI! FUCK ALL YOU HATERS!

62482206
>hey guise whats dis aviator coin im totally not stixil looking for more poor suckers as exit liq from my 3 year old scam

62482270
>>62482206
I doubt that's the case. Magic definitely used to shill here and I'm almost certain the first threads were from him with all the "insider" shit he would post. There's simply no more liquidity to farm from this board. It's dead along with crypto.

62482398
>>62482029
Unless we break past $1m again, I’m not falling for this fake pump. It’s probably the same front running bots that come and go

62482721
>+52%
>$5k volume
Yea I’m definitely falling for that one. BTC is slowing down again which means a market wide dump is about to happen. Avi is going back to $400k. Check em

62482861
this coin is driving me insane

62482868
>>62482861
Why don't you play some FUD Free Fox in the aviator arcade if you need to blow off some steam?

62482872
>>62481704
>2023 and early 2024 were comfy

not really (sure the chart went up), they hedged way too much with Base and they ghosted the fuck out of them. the shitposting was fun

>>62482868
ill play that penguin game too

>>62481415
>$300k mcap
that's nothing, if it hits 5-10m without any massive selloff to 1m ill be interested

62483375
>>62482872
I acknowledge that the team messed up along the way but it was still a comfy hold early on. The tg was chill, the team was active, the AMAs were something to look forward to, and overall everyone had hope. Then all the “early frens and supporters” and paw drama started happening, the kerasu saga, the obvious signs they gave up on the arcade in favor of the bridge that no one cared for, the on chain summer BS, ambiguous messages trying to lead us on, etc. It just continued going down hill from there.

62483861
kek the fake pump did t even hold for 24 hours. I remember them lasting for at least a few days, even a couple months before they dumped. This isn’t looking so good

62484514
kek jimmy won.
matthew red won.
cryptoempress won.
rowdy randy won.
maxpain won.
sumdog won.
shadowlord won.
aviator lost. airfox? LMAO you have no liquidity. VC abandoned stratus. Remember the 1 cent per AVI? LOL. magic is gone btw? is mitch gone? is stixil the only one that remains? holders quit their jobs at tim hortons for this coin and THIS is how you repay them?

62484570
don't listen to the haters stixil. If you keep trying you will eventually succeed and show everyone was wrong. I do suggest putting back the liquidity, its a huge red flag taking the liq out due to lack of funds as it signals users you are out of money which could lead to team token sales to fund the project coming from the communities pockets.

62484686
>>62484514
Magic hasn’t been in the tg for probably a year now? It’s said he hangs out in discord but the discord seemed dead last time I checked. He was also supposedly heavily involved with V2s development. I don’t know who is left anymore because I can’t even join the tg.

>>62484570
>don't listen to the haters stixil. If you keep trying you will eventually succeed and show everyone was wrong
Falko? Anyways, I ask in good faith. Besides the obvious trolling, what has been said so far that is wrong? Stixil clearly lacks any direction for where he wants to take this project. He has jumped from one idea to another any time something doesn’t work out. When V2 launched and got no traction, he immediately announced that they will take over stratus and for us to ‘stay tuned’. When that eventually doesn’t work out, he will have already came up with another new thing to keep people hanging on. It’s been like that for the last 3 years and it will continue being that way for as long as avi is around

62484716
>>62481088
They rebranded, increased token supply for themselves and airdropped everyone the new tokens.

62485941
>>62483861
>the fake pump did t even hold for 24 hours
I hope no one thought that mini pump was organic. It is just one of many that happened in the last couple of years and it’s probably a tg member trying to farm liquidity. It’s sad that this is what the volume is based on. Some bot / poorfag trying to farm $10-$50 from some other poorfags who got baited into buying a green chart lol

62486102
where is the fucking liquidity i cant sell what the fuck i need my money now. airfox? really? where is my cent per AVI that was promised to me

62486284
>>62486102
There is $11k liquidity. Thats more than enough

62486513
>$300k mcap
Yea I’m thinking it’s over

62486525
>>62486513
$80m whale is starting to sell. A former six figure bag is now worth $4k. Ouch

62487100
>>62484514
The Schizo also won and the red baron roped.
Flawless Victory

>>62481067
>>62481088
Anon, crypto is dead just as this board.
can't you fucking tell this already after two years?
The days of making 5 figs or even 3 figs on ETH shitcoins is over.
retail sadly migrated to solana and crime is legal now
those days of quick money on ETH and biz shitcoins are not coming back

62487573
>>62486525
a few bags were worth 7 figs at the height (yeah yeah, muh liquidity, I know) silly fuckers held all this time, that's gotta hurt, a few got out with 6 figs but fucked the chart

62487601
>>62486284
whoa, wtf? hadn't even spotted that, 9k now.....pffffffhahahahah

62487664
>>62487100
Solana is part of why crypto got fucked imo. Outside of the scammers and a few degens, I can’t imagine too many people making money there. But yea I don’t see any light at the end of the tunnel for crypto as a whole

62489045
Damn we hit $200k. Which one of you fucks is selling?

62489432
>>62481067
If you haven't noticed, anon, jannies and jeets wrung every drop of juice out of this board for years. I still come back on occasion and reply out of habit, just to find the majority of my posts are the end of a thread. /biz/ is a fucking ghost town

62489758
Wait a minute tho, if he does actually launch this token launcher etc for robin hood, it could be one of the early launches and go to the moon

62489887
>>62489432
>just to find the majority of my posts are the end of a thread
lol I thought it was just me. I’ll go into what seems like an active thread and as soon as I post a reply, that shit dies. It’s weird

>>62489758
>robinhood
I’m very skeptical about sentiment going back to what it was. I honestly don’t think crypto will ever be a space where we see 100x’s across the board. Even 2024s run was shit and I don’t think we will see that either. I see robinhood as another Base type of thing. Granted, Base did pretty good in the beginning but it ultimately turned out to be a chain for insider trading. That’s why crypto is in the state that it’s in. Nothing but a bunch of jews

62489964
>>62489045
>down 27% off less than 4k volume
yeah this shit is cooked.

item.title: Buy SPCX. $135 is the absolute floor. It will be at $260 within 12 months. You will hate yourself if you don't get in no… — 29 replies — 116.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Buy SPCX. $135 is the absolute floor. It will be at $260 within 12 months. You will hate yourself if you don't get in no…

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-07-16 19:30 UTC

OP

Buy SPCX. $135 is the absolute floor. It will be at $260 within 12 months. You will hate yourself if you don't get in now.

Early replies (first 3)

62483359
>>62483350
4%

62483366
>>62483359
?

62483376
>>62483366
!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62483884
>>62483350
>buy my bags

62483890
>OP bought the top

62484127
>>62483350
>Confidence Level: 50%

62484135
>>62483350
>Weighted Base = Anal Consensus

62484142
>>62483350
so why isnt this company taking loans to buy spcx if its gonna go up 86%

62484426
>>62483350
what a lovely graphic. i'm convinced to all-in my life savings now

62484437
>>62483350
i was told last week 150 was the floor
what will the floor be next week? 110?

62484447
Who is buying starlink? Airplanes care barely afford jet fuel, let alone WiFi on a plane. Where is there a need for more internet access? 3rd world countries already have free WiFi everywhere and use WhatsApp. lol lmao

62484508
>>62483350
This picture is AI generated.

62485058
>>62484508
Wow u dont say! Basterd u must ape into life savings, based wyt dev wont rug blody!

62485272
>>62483350
lmao what a shitty stock

62485350
>>62483350
Why buy now when I can get it for $70 in a couple months?

62485635
>>62484437
Smug frog

62486516
>>62483366
he doesn't know
kek good goy

62486545
>>62483350
No it won't, kessler syndrome hasn't been solved. And until you can guarantee that Earth's orbit won't turn into a wall of scrap moving at several times the speed sound, trapping us on the planet. The whole space thing is nothing but speculation. Anyone with enough rocket tech to get to low earth orbit can make space exploration impossible. Wake me when the orbital defenses get built.

62486606
>>62483366
Good goyim

62486621
>>62483350
>this company which isn't remotely profitable should actually be worth 4 trillions
>the present p/s of 104 is no big deal
>let's make it 200+ instead
just lol. spcx is a 50 bucks stock.

62486626
>>62486545
how do we clean up the mess? planetes-style crews working overtime?

62486686
>>62486621
literally all they need to do is capture one asteroid and it's to the moon

62486692
>>62486686
SpaceX to the moon!

62486741
it is likely to go lower once the early investors can sell their stock

62487453
>>62484142
that would be a conflict of interest of course
>>62484127
basically guaranteed
>>62484447
rural areas

62487473
>>62483350
Hahaha you boughted the IPO?
Sorry man, not buying your bag until it's 40~60.

62487479
Pre IPO I thought ultimately the reason why the float was low was so they could clandestinely support a level until the lockups are done.
Haven't changed my mind yet but that number might not be 135.

62488213
>>62483350
not buying your shitty stock

62489975
>>62486692

item.title: Chainlink influencers panic as Link nowhere — 47 replies — 65.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Chainlink influencers panic as Link nowhere

Open thread • Replies: 47 • Updated: 2026-07-16 18:47 UTC

OP

to be found in much anticipated DTCC announcement. Apparently it's another conspiracy...

Early replies (first 3)

62484148
now ask yourself this, what would your reaction be if the shoe was on the other foot?

in other words, if the dtcc post had quoted chainlink accounts instead of xrp? kind of odd isn't it?

62484149
Peel thread

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62484375
>>62484287
>This is obviously free engagement from the retard army

bro the post was made by the actual dtcc.


> we know that chainlink is integrated and XRPL isnt

no one cares about that buzzword shit boy. when it comes to cl, all of their so called integrations and partnerships and live products are marketing gimmicks

62484378
>>62484292
>The tweets arent about XRP, just from XRP accounts.


agreed. the question is what would the cult reaction be if they had instead come from chainlink kol accounts

fyi i dont care about your gay semantic tech arguments with xrp cult

62484400
>>62484375
>bro the post was made by the actual dtcc.
Yes now its being spread heavily by the XRP community, its actually great marketing and doesn't imply that the DTCC isnt even using XRPL (because they arent). They have posted on their own website what networks they support.

>no one cares about that buzzword shit boy
https://www.dtcc.com/news/2026/may/12/dtcc-collaborates-with-chainlink-to-advance-24-7-collateral-management

Can we see XRPLs integration news?

>instead come from chainlink kol accounts
It really doesn't matter what accounts it came from.

62484425
Its actually pretty funny they didnt use any tweets from community accounts that are confirmed to be integrated. Its next level trolling.

62484443
>>62484143
>>62484160
>>62484176

Remember when Adem/Thomas used to pretend he wasn't a xerpie?
So glad you went mask off at last, that's one layer peeled at least.

62484554
kek

62485182
https://www.dtcc.com/news/2026/july/15/dtcc-turns-tokenization-into-reality

lol
lmao

62485195
>>62485182
my village is hungry, when can chainlink feed us?

62485437
>>62484378
>the question is what would the cult reaction be if they had instead come from chainlink kol accounts
What is even the point of this question? Spit it out, vagueboy

62486773
>>62485182
But...... uhhhh............ price?? Oh.....and... chart???

62487540
>>62484150
it will never not be funny that every single fudkek out there has turned out, without exception, to be a lifeless husk with several addictions and arrested development. the cream of the crop, this fud entourage.

62487577
>>62487540
as opposed to linktards who are living a fullfilling life

62487617
>>62487577
actually, yes, we do
>posts screencap of a fudster larping as broken linkie
yawn. do you keep saving these? kek

62487623
>>62487617
90% of comments under chainlink twitter are broke baggies complaining about the price but whatever, i'm sure they're all Adem larping as a Linkie right?

62487634
>>62487623
>twitter
There is your problem

62487662
>>62487634
there's barely 10 Linkies on this board, the whole community is on Twitter

62487867
>>62487662
The Linkies here are the ones you are interacting with, not sure what the relevance of these twatter fags is.

62487873
>>62487867
the few linkies here are insane and angry all the time. They believe their rank 18 altcoin is being suppressed. Are these the "people" I should trust?

62487955
>>62487577
>>62487623
>>62487662
>>62487873
someone's sad his gay little fud thread didn't go as planned

>Link nowhere to be found in much anticipated DTCC announcement
OOF

62487976
>>62487955
Goodmorning sirs, nobody cares about your single digit Indian token. This isnt 2021 Rajesh. Thankyou and goodbye

62487984
>>62487976
>cares enough to tell me he doesn't care

this is like a catch-22 but in reverse

62488191
>>62485182
uh oh

62488201
Xrp isn’t involved with the DTCC in any way whatsoever. They were mentioned many years ago but nothing with the recent blockchain developments.
You should read Chainlink gods recent chat with Larsen were he admitted xrp’s only use case is to fund ripplelabs other ventures. Larsen also claimed that when they sell xrp it’s good for holders because it gets cheaper for baggies to buy.
You couldn’t make it up.
Are ripple still going to replace swift?

62488454
>>62487873
You are free to leave for twitter, nobody is asking you to post here.

62488674
>>62487623
considering how much time and resources have been spent here for a literal decade, i'd wager that is 100x more likely that a fudkek is creating yet another twatter account to larp as a hurt ex-linkie

also you are brown and poor and old and sick lmao

62488700
>>62488674
imagine if he put this much effort into fixing his diseased stomach instead of fudding link full time. i wouldn't be surprised if it causes him undue stress that exacerbates his condition

62488789
>>62488674
goodmorning sir

how's your stinky mouth going? Did you cash out your Link and drink some cow piss to cure the infection? I've heard that its a great remedy in Uttar Pradesh

62489465
>>62488789
Thats just a variation of the "my mom is kicking me out[..] should I give her X LINK tokens?" post, you stupid brown druggie old cunt.

62489483
>>62489465
>sir it's all FUD by the ugly bulgarian porn addicts don't believe
You shitskins stick out like a sore thumb by the way you post and write

The fact that you shill an dinosaur altcoin in 2026 is a dead giveaway you're a poor shitskin trying to /makeit/ because you were too poor to buy Bitcoin. Go and play with trains or whatever you jeets do

62489723
>>62488674
>>62489465
lol I can smell the chicken tikka masala from here. With all the money CLL has I thought they could hire someone better

item.title: Who's still buying BTC at this level? — 43 replies — 58.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Who's still buying BTC at this level?

Open thread • Replies: 43 • Updated: 2026-07-16 18:47 UTC

OP

BTC has already come a long way. Are you still buying here, waiting for a pullback, or just holding what you already have?

>Curious what everyone's plan is.

Early replies (first 3)

62475243
waiting for $10k and I will buy 3 btc

62475257
>>62475235
YOUR MOTHER

62475258
>>62475235
I'm DCAing bitcoin at the second half of the midterm year.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62477948
>>62477616
What makes you think that?

62477951
>>62477616
I know him in real life, he's loaded.

62479061
>>62477951
Ben??

62479203
>>62475971
When 10k or 5k

62479569
>>62475235
Time to buy bitcoin

62480040
>>62479203
delusional peg

62480706
>>62477951
You are him.

62480931
im buying its the cheapest it will ever get!

62481737
>>62480931
it will never go cheap

62481842
buy now

62483293
>>62481737
What's the best entry buying

62483295
Look for Robinhood chainnnnnnnnnn

62483495
>>62476425
You're broke.

62483652
>>62483495
broke and broken hearted

62483662
>>62475235
Tomorrow will be a nice day for buying, he will dump it just like the last time

62484050
>>62483662
Short-term moves don't bother me much anymore. I'll keep accumulating and natively staking my BTC.

62484717
>>62484050
Do you have your BTC on a TBV? or how are you staking it natively?

62486316
>>62483293
Wait for 60k

62486462
>>62475258
been DCA at $100/week for a while. Total spent is $17,400 and total value is $17,888.

62486528
>>62483293
DCAing into Bitcoin in the second half of a midterm year usually works out pretty well. Remember, just knowing we're in a bear market isn't enough, you have to take action. You aren't going to time the exact bottom, but slowly accumulating when prices are low is how you navigate bear markets.

62486684
>>62484717
TBVs are still in the public testnet phase on Aave V4. Hopefully we'll see a launch later this year.

62487150
>>62486684
It's a boring market

62487536
>>62487150
How do you know? It's still in testnet. They're literally testing the use cases. Plenty of potential if it works.

62488078
>>62486528
You're making good money.

62488091
>>62486462
Time to take profit

62488156
>>62475235
at 40k yeah

62488664
>>62487536
We'll see. More ways to use native BTC are what will matter in the long run.

62488668
>>62488156
not possible

62489527
>>62483295
New hype chain lol

62489716
>>62475235
ahem

item.title: I've been holding LINK since october 2017 (two man team, nail salon etc...) back then I was full of hope and optimism. — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've been holding LINK since october 2017 (two man team, nail salon etc...) back then I was full of hope and optimism.

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-07-16 19:23 UTC

OP

I've been holding LINK since october 2017 (two man team, nail salon etc...) back then I was full of hope and optimism. The board was fantastic, the memes, the energy everything seemed so perfect. I could feel my dreams coming true in a matter of years. 2020-2021 were probably the best years I've ever had and my portfolio peaked at roughly 700k.

Fast forward to 2026 and its been a total disaster. I dread living and I barely have 85k in chainlink and some other altcoins that rugged. I don't visit biz anymore because its super boring and slow. I have a monotonous consulting job at a Big 4 which is spiritually crushing me, I can barely get out of bed thinking about going to my office. Over the last months I started drinking quite a bit, mostly gin to cope. Don't know if its because of the bear market or my job. I'm trying to cut down on it but its really difficult, its probably genetic since my father was borderline alcoholic. By the time I get home, all I can think about is my first drink. Then my second etc... Wagies at my workplace tell me I'm doing well but I'm spiritually dead, I wish I wasn't alive and I can't take it anymore. I seriously don't want to work this shitty wagie goy job until I'm 70. I simply want to be free.

I thought that by now Chainlink would have been atleast triple digits and would have allowed me to escape wageslavery, yet here we are at 8 dollars. The DTCC and SWIFT news is great but it didn't affect the price, I keep hearing about random dates being thrown but I won't get my hopes up because all the previous ones turned out to be nothingburgers.

Does anybody have a real timeframe in which we will see some positive price action? I mean serious answers not memes. I'm tired

>inb4 just 10 more years bro

Early replies (first 3)

62489117
>>62489081
damn bro just about same story here
i do think eventually we'll win and we'll look back on these times and laugh
the fact im 100% remote now really helps. fuck commuting to a gay office

62489257
DR;NS

62489623
>>62489081
you should give me the rest of you link, it will free you and awaken you spiritually

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62489732
by the end of the year you'll be chugging entire bottles of gin when link is trading at $3

62489764
HOLY SHIT

By sheer chance I happened to be watching Sunny today, "The gang finds a dumpster baby", and recognised one of the frames Asslarper used (pic related).

In this episode, Dennis (AB's avatar) convinces an activist to CHAINLINK himself to a tree, then LITERALLY cuckolds him.

>A SINGULAR TREE
>THE cuckOLDS OF CRYPTO!!!

Now do you believe me?

Inverse Assblaster; what did he tell you was useless?

62489799
>>62489081
>Does anybody have a real timeframe in which we will see some positive price action? I mean serious answers not memes. I'm tired
DTCC's event was a soft launch for a day for a several institutions. They go live in October. It's between that and staking and SWIFT you're waiting on.

62489814
>>62489799
>new meme date just dropped
what happened to the previous ones sar?

62489836
>>62489799
Staking is the real one that matters

62489856
>>62489836
Staking is applying security on something that needs it. Banks needs it.

62489939

item.title: As I was saying yesterday. The Chinese are slowly giving the news to trading partners. No more dollars. You want to buy… — 13 replies — 25.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
As I was saying yesterday. The Chinese are slowly giving the news to trading partners. No more dollars. You want to buy…

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-07-16 19:00 UTC

OP

As I was saying yesterday. The Chinese are slowly giving the news to trading partners. No more dollars. You want to buy from us. You get a Yuan swap line. You settle trade imbalances in gold or Yuan and use our settlement systems.

What can the US do? All they have is threats against the likes of Argentina. China has the vital goods they need at the right prices. The will continue to do this and kill dollar dominance.

Theres no other choice or enter an inflationary spiral.

Early replies (first 3)

62488083
how does this play out in 20 years? half the countries buy using Yuan? US goes back to manufacturing cuz dollar falls? Americans finally lose weight?

62488086
>>62488026
I thought Milei was anti-Chinese?

62488137
>Argentine peso devalues to zimbabwe-tier levels
>Hard for businesses to even buy USD because the market is so shallow and the currency so unwanted price discovery becomes tricky
>Milei's lolbertarian stance does little to appease markets, forex remains tightly regulated to avoid massive capital flights
>country with almost no industry or advanced services to export
>USD reserves run dry
>Great opportunity for China to dump it's surplus production, will probably be selling shit to Argentina at a loss in a government-set artificial exchange rate just as they do with Veneuela and African shitholes that struggle to access USD
>brown thirdie posts his paid shilling painting this as a massive victory for china and a sign of the dollar collapsing
We should have flags on /biz/. I'd call you a chink but I don't think chinks can even access this site.
>As I was saying yesterday.
Your thread died within 24hs on one of the slowest boards on this site. That should tell you something. Fuck off paid brown tankie.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62488144
>>62488083
Another moron who thinks the yuan is convertible into money

62488151
>>62488137
seething + coping + increasingly demographically brown migger who can't win wars

62488166
>>62488151

He’s real mad

62488178
America will nuke you bitter fags into the stone age before letting China rule this gay earth

62488182
Why do I care if one thirdie shithole wants to use yuan again?

62488221
China is going to launch a gold-backed trade currency. That's why they have been piling up gold like frat boys on a drunk cheerleader. Also opening Shanghai metals market, just recently the Hong Kong gold exchange, and Singapore coming online soon. The USD became king because it was backed by gold. Then the USA got greedy and cheated. It will lose its throne to gold.

62488302
>>62488144
it's convertible to gold though

62488330
>>62488221
You will be nuked.

62489746
>>62488302
LOL
LMAO
you go ahead and try to do that and get back to us

62489793
>>62488026
>What can the US do?
It's called the petrodollar, anon, not the stuffdollar.

item.title: The bottom of bottoms — 3 replies — 5.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
The bottom of bottoms

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-07-16 18:59 UTC

OP

You guys have the opportunity to enter the bottom. Look at picrel, it's fucking dry as ur mothers pussy.

>How do i get in?
Go to libertyswap (google u dumb fuck) and turn usdc, usdt or eth into the native token PLS (pulsechain). It takes like a few sec, you don't need to bridge ur coins.

>Now what?
Head over to pulsex, when the native dex. It will try to change your RPC, i recommend chainlist (google again dumb fuck 3th worlder nigga) look for the RPC settings

>Got it
Now that you're connected to Pulsechain, swap PLS for Hex

>How do i leave?
Well, vice versa. Swap Hex for USDC and head back to libertyswap.

>Whats Libertyswap?
A Dex that allows you to swap coins without using a bridge

>Whats Pulsex?
The native Dex

>Why is it a pain in the ass???!
Cause the founder Richard Heart is a notorious scammer that doesn't want you to scoop the bottom. He realized hex peaked back in 2021 and was overloaded with whales, so he had to go on a fudding spree and make everything possible to get people out.

>Why should i get in rn?
Cause it's a dead fucking shitcoin as of right now and any buy turns the community into chimping out apes, it's the bottom of FUCKING OVERSOLD NOBODY GIVES A FUCK ABOUT THIS DEAD SCAM SHITCOIN bottom.

Here you go bros. Easiest 10x ever.

I HATE ALL OF YOU

Early replies (first 3)

62487217
here the RPC setting you absolute trash piece of dumb fuck hobo nigga moron

62487235
>>62487210
Buy the schueler coin blady basterrrrd!

62489785
Buy buy buy

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: both Swift and DTCC are going live right now — 157 replies — 74.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
both Swift and DTCC are going live right now

Open thread • Replies: 157 • Updated: 2026-07-16 17:24 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62466455
Price?

62466457
>>62466455
Suppressed,

62466461
And literal top-buying turned full time fudders are finally capitulating.

Imagine subjecting yourself to all the pain and never seeing it through the end. I gave myself 6 months after SWIFT and DTCC are live to see if the token shoots up, and then decide wether to sell half my stack of just staking rewards.

But to sell RIGHT BEFORE the literal ANNOUNCED DATES of the two biggest financial behemoths' launches? That's crazy

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62485247
>>62466457
Youre mentally ill if you think bitcoin went down because of link. Holy shit this level of derangement is seriously scary. Dudes like you are actually out there roaming the streets along with me. Absolute mindboggling stuff

All coincidences.

62486018

62486630

you'd think linkies would be more enthused. maybe deep down they know this means nothing

62487460
>>62486630

62487501
>>62466645

62487520
>>62467154
You need to look into how much failed trx cost banks each year. That alone is a huge incentive. Then there’s also a huge suit of frictionless financial products.
Kek fuddies

62487535
>>62487501

that was the fat slimy kike David, not Brad, but yeah, he literally told people
>well XRP dropping like a stone is GOOD ackshually because... you can buy more at a cheaper price!!
>XRP is not a sercurity. Ripple's XRP is Ripple's to use to advance their business interests. If you'd prefer exposure to all of Ripple's business, you can buy stock in Ripple.

62487575
>>62487501

62487582
>>62487535

sirs XRP is bad token, please invest in Chainlink if you want the village well fed.

62487621

62488189

62488200

62488218
>>62488211
one day chainlink will pump to one million and we will all be rich. I believe!

62488229
>>62488211
fuck you bastard bich

62488315

62488326

?

62488334

62488492

they are outpacing the buys with dumps. you still are dancing around that you absolute imbecile

62488527

62488547
>>62488527

62488569
>>62488547

62488802

please the village is hungry

62489225

Open thread • Replies: 161 • Updated: 2026-07-16 17:15 UTC

OP

Sell SPCX you fools. Sell until it's too late.

Early replies (first 3)

62466535
They should just make corporate espionage legal in western countries at this point. The only people who are winning from the current laws are the Chinese

62466548

lol

62466549
>>62466546

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62482048

>kills people
kek what a neat nation

62482165
>>62480305
The AI part of the company hides the insanely profitable launch part.
Gotta wait and see how profitable AI ends up being. Same with Anthropic and OpenAI.

62482168
>>62466549
Newhalf porn(?)

62482212
>>62482165
I think you're overselling its ai division. spacex is using models for materials research, starship design, research purposes exclusively. spacex will never make money directly from ai, and grok is not property of the company.

62484783
>>62466527
Below IPO price today ($132.70). I think $70 entry is interesting.

62485150
>>62466527
I kneel.

62485163
>>62485150
135 is the floor

62485166
I'm going to laugh so hard when Elon gets liquidated.

62485353
>>62466527
thanks just sold

62485386
>>62480129
even /sp/ has em jannies killed this board this thread is 5 days old

62485402
>>62466548
the chinese are pros at reverse engineering western tech. im sure they figured it out from all the footage spacex offers of their launches for free plus any spies they have in the company.

62485724
>>62485163
Sure

62485794
Basically I'm just not gonna sell. I know.. UGH I know.. IM SORRY! It's just that I'm not selling is all.

62485982
>>62467819
China produces a fuck ton of terrestrial based communication already to the point that it became a western security issue.
They are moving into space with the same tech they are certainly not lacking in capability.

62486000
>>62467844
There is an infinite amount of real, accurate data in the real world through cameras, microphones and various other sensors, alongside human communication and interaction.

AI doesn't need to run via text alone.

62486015
>>62485982
How many Chinese 5G towers in your country?

62486340
>>62468867
Bbc, german tvs, etc

62486345
>>62466527
They hodled

62486410
>>62466549
Nintendo

62486555
>>62486340
>Being a eurocuck
Sorry, I can't relate.

62486873
What about other space stocks, I'm pretty deep on rocket labs and lunr

62487093
>>62485402
the chinese version is essentially a giant net that it lands in, they don't catch the rocket like spacex does

62487201
Why does it matter that SPCX's float is only 4%? Explain like I'm a retard.

62487937
>>62486873
RKLB is smart in the way they position themselves around SPCX. They integrate vertically, have good connections to gov and biz and deliver launches. I keep them as secondary launch provider for redundancy reasons. And I hold.
LUNR is hit or miss (their fancy missions either succeed or fail) and I see neither the vision nor the ability or capacity to become more than that. Would I send payload with them? Only for the experience, not if it's something essential or expensive. I'll likely sell them at one point.

62487989
>>62466527
Most implessive ching chong ping ling don din baow

62488352
>>62487937
So are you still holding lunr?
It's been bleeding for a month.
Would you hold any other space stocks?

Other than silver miners idk where to store my cash

62488426
>>62487989
Unironically

62488803
>>62488352
>still holding lunr?
Because I bought it low (still in the green for me) and not too much of it.
>any other space stocks
SPCX for the long game, averaging in over the next years, I'm a customer since years and trust them a lot.
RKLB because they're solid, also for the long game. Unsure whether I'd buy today, but I'd be a customer w/o question.
NASDAQ:FLY has potential. They do good work, have a good track record. But they're niche and I'm not sure whether they can expand or even hold their position.
I bought LSE:SSIT (an investment trust that can invest in private equity) 3 years ago, but the management is woke (I met them) and they're opportunistic, not big game. Some like it, I don't.
That said, I'm more interested in private equity and cannot really give stock advice, my focus and expertise is also too narrow.

62489130
>>>>>impying SpaceX is a space company
Thanks for the AI hype money pit money, suckers.
@Grok how do I kill myself?
>>62487201
shitcoin with 96% of the coins already owned by the devs and a cabal of early investors that will dump on your ass soon

62489163
>>62467782
You save on weight like this and it's more consistent. There's a reason the early Starship renders had those big ass legs that were quietly swapped for light chopsticks later.

item.title: I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. — 222 replies — 28.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job.

Open thread • Replies: 222 • Updated: 2026-07-16 11:49 UTC

OP

I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. I'm not young, 40 next year, and don't have a job history or references. High school graduate.
How fucked am I?

Early replies (first 3)

62449979
I live in California, too.

62449982
What did you do for the last 23 years? Hard to help without more context. Do you have skills?

62449989
>>62449982
Depression and NEET means not in education employment or training.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62474892
>>62474884
the creature making the OP does not want advice. It's doing an act for (you)s
This is actually good advice to someone so defeated they can't find jobs felons on the sex offender registry get after 15 years binned up.
I'm sure a free AI could coach a defeated NEET into one of those jobs.
OP isn't though, just a weird fuck

62475051
>>62474884
I think this is the first time I've seen someone call Ai like chat gpt an "LLM". Well, I guess I've got nothing better to do.

>>62474892
Get a life you fucking loser. Dogshit like you doesn't deserve even the attention I've given you already.

62475932
>>62475051
>Well, I guess I've got nothing better to do.
Repeating myself, but the modern human needs to get comfortable using LLMs to be productive and successful. Avoid falling in to emotional or political traps involving them. View them as a useful tool. A human who refuses to use a calculator would be less productive than a human who does use a calculator.

The same is true for LLMs. Don't blindly trust everything it tells you. Get a feel for how to prompt it to give you the results you want and double check things that you aren't sure about. A lot of people on /biz/ and in general seethe over LLMs, call you a pajeet if you mention them etc, just ignore them. I'm a professional making 6 figs a year in an white collar position, I use LLMs every single day outside of work and inside of work.

As a skill-less NEET who doesn't know how to navigate the world it's the most invaluable tool at your disposal.

62475990
>>62449961
very white european of you

62476030
>>62475932
You put way too much faith in an algorithm poorly masquerading as ai. If it's a tool it should be a learning tool, not a crutch.

62476048
>>62476030
Nowhere did I say it's a crutch, I very clearly said it's a tool. You're starting to sound like you're beyond helping desu. Good luck.

62476501
>>62453688
The fact that you think there is any significant amount of “attacks” in this thread and not genuine criticism shows how far removed you are from the world. It’s going to be a tough couple years for you as you enter the workforce. You’ve received tons of honest advice.

62476747
>>62457849
Anon can we talk on private? I think I can help you with this job for a fraction of 5% of what you get paid. I am desperately in need of money right now.

62477511
OP is a mentally ill retard who is in denial about everything, or maybe just a troll.

ignore this thread.

62478296
i am in the exact same position as op, but i am 24 and have an undergrad in history
how do i even get a job, and i dont wanna work in a fucking school

62478683
>>62449961
I think the French Foreign legion will still take you.

Otherwise, you could save some McWagie dollars and go to a trade school. Plumbers, machinists, and other tech trades are in demand.

62479116
>>62478296
If you're this lost the anon who said "talk to chat GPT" had the correct advice for you. Scroll up and read it. An AI can easily guide you through the process of finding a starting job to build up. Any decent job wants to know you will show up every morning, gotta start low.
You can scroll up to my post, also. That advice applies to you. At 24 you will have more hope than a 40 year old though.
I would even recommend fast food at that age as you can actually fit in with the young folks who work at those jobs.
>>62478683
Trade school is a bust. If you are actually handy and been doing carpentry and repairing your own stuff since you were a teenager then you likely would have slid into this type of job already. Good luck getting some tradie boss to tolerate you if you don't know left from right. Seriously. Better off starting as a laborer and getting your hands dirty that way first, then moving into a trade.

62480858
What the hell kind of cleaner do you use on painted wood paneling?

62480965
>>62478296
>>have an undergrad in history
>>i dont wanna work in a fucking school
how did you even graduate if you are too lazy to even read the answers in this thread?

62480999
>>62450695
try doing yardwork around the neighborhood. remember that old people still think you're a young, strapping lad. plenty of people would rather pay a neighbor to mow their lawn or powerwash their driveway, and from there you can network and get a regular list of clients.

62481011
>>62450832
yeah, the reason is it's harder to exploit people who would rather not work. give them too much shit and they just quit. employers like people they can abuse because it makes them feel important.

62481052
>>62451705
you need to start drinking raw milk and eat rare grassfed steak every day. also limit coffee and soft drinks, and take vitamins especially extra calcium. your cavities will stop in their tracks and your teeth will harden. if you have exposed pulp you can cover it with biodentine, but that's kind of a pain in the ass so hopefully you can avoid it.

t. haven't needed a dentist in 15 years.

62481814
>>62479116
the clanker says i can work in a museum, for content creation, PR, HR or as a writer, these all are too broad
honestly i would like to live abroad in europe if i am to work on the food sector. maybe a cafe in paris or something

it also says i should look on linkedin. does that actually work? everyone i know says its a humiliation ritual
>>62480965
i graduated because of that, i only check 4chins and le internets like twice a day for 30 minutes max, chud

62482383
>>62480858
Nevermind, you repaint that shit, apparently.

62483153
>>62481814
because you have a degree you can get into cushy government jobs, eventually. If you're some sort of DEI you have a better chance for direct hire but reality it is you need experience. Fries in the bag fren, the clanker will give you the right advice if you tell if you need local advice for jobs that often hire with no experience or work history. You're young, put some faith in yourself and try something harder if you want, people hire for hard work constantly due to turnover.
Museum? Unlikely, but if you have a real passion and somehow charm them in the hiring interview (and so how get invited to one...) I guess maybe eventually.

62483326
I have become so accustomed to laidback NEET like living that i don't know if i even have the attention span to focus for 8 hours of work every day

62483345
>>62483326
Depending on your early life and early adulthood, maybe.
Hopefully you did some difficult things when you were a kid/teenager and showed up to school on time. Showing up is the first step to working, and a history of showing up is what will help an employer believe you will be there 5 days a week (the first step to getting hired to a good job.)
If you're still a young adult and not too far removed from your school days, you still have all those years of waking up and making it to class in your habits.
Hopefully you did things like long hard hikes, picking fruit all day for cash, biking around for hours at a time trying to keep up with the older kids, reading long novels in binge sessions (this one's a push, but it is maybe some hope.) Doing any of this may be an indicator you have some push in you. Hopefully you do even if you haven't pushed yourself much.

There are jobs where you barely need to focus. The work comes at you, and you do it. If you just shrug it off the supervisors will give you some pushes, and if you don't answer the calls, serve the customers, pick the weeds, you'll be let go.
You might think landscaping is so bad, but you get driven around by the crew boss, maybe a couple funny lads to joke around with, and you just gotta get it done when it's time to unload and work. Back in the truck and off to the next job. It's broken up, it's not so bad. You shed weight, get a lot of sun and hopefully some chipper lads to laugh with.

If going to work doesn't risk your benefits, you could give it a try. For young people (24 and under) fastfood can be great. You'll be around people like you all day, and being close to people is the number one way to develop relationships. It's not always busy, but they'll always have something for you to be doing, which may challenge your attention. Specific advice is higher up in the thread with more suggestions of workplaces

62483372
>>62449989
>>62449979
>>62449961
go to university (probably free as low income)
do TAing (they will pay you for this)
graduate and they'll just look at most recent job history (TAing)

nobody will care about before then

62483927
>>62483153
museums here absolutely demand a masters in return for minimum wage (about $1200/month before tax)

62483949
>>62457593
>How is that a bad thing? Thats a good thing.
Only a good thing if you've been working at least 5-10yr and have somr capital. If you're broke, you have to hope others share the robotics and AI wealth

62484177
>>62451837
Best advice ITT
Being a janitor is comfy and they will take anybody. Same as a garbageman, pay is probably better for that too
At 40 with no job history, I would do it

62484243
>>62469039
Kek you can always tell someone is an asshole that has problems getting along with people when they complain about everything.

62484364
>>62450482
You could say you were a Christian missionary for two decades and apply at in-n-out. Solid gold.

62484820
>>62484243
well he did say he was french

>>62484364
>christian
>in-n-out
you mean chick-fil-a

62487921
Anyway, life sucks then you give up.

item.title: Let me guess, you NEED more? — 76 replies — 27.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Let me guess, you NEED more?

Open thread • Replies: 76 • Updated: 2026-07-16 16:46 UTC

OP

Let me guess, you NEED more?

Early replies (first 3)

62477627
Can I fuck the long haired pale ghost that lives in there?

62477639
>>62477619
...Bug infested dilapidated Shack on dirt...
>Bug infested dilapidated Shack on dirt in JAPAN

62477707
Correct.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62482563
>>62478389
And just like that I will never set foot in rural Japan

62482582
>>62482047
Yes, it is unfortunate.
Just me and my waifu, in our scenic peaceful home in the inaka.
I wonder what we can do together to pass the time?

62482591
I personally need more, yes

62483098
>>62477790
This is mostly how it actually works. Nip zoning and build codes are really permissive for old properties. Nips don't like old houses so they're cheap.
But the downside of this is you're assuming risk. So if you buy even a nice cheap old house in Japan that's underneath some of the red tape that preclude outright demolition and rebuilding you're taking on the risk of just being up shit creak if the house ever gets hit by a typhoon, burns down, or an earthquake cracks your foundation. Because if any damage is done to the house that necessitates rebuilding basically in practice you can't rebuild. Then you have to baghold an empty lot with punitive taxes.

62484201
japanese property might be cheap, but isn't life there expensive, and the wages absolute crap?

62484253
>>62477639
this unjacklly

62484315
>>62484201
life is cheap, but wages are crap and income taxes are higher
the best situation is to work remotely for good pay where you are not double taxed on income, like me
Wages have been improving for regular Japanese, but the tax brackets haven't moved, so effectively they are earning less adusted for inflation
Everyone is mad about it and wants the tax brackets adjusted for inflation

62484348
>>62478792
Sent, ranjeesh i hope your mother get her surgery and she's able to walk again ;)

62484382
>>62477619
Getting the visa is the tough part

62484699
>>62477619
>rural japan
>"rural"
>japan

Go find me a 2x2km box on google earth in japan with nothing in it.

Rural Japan is dogshit because its like Southern NH on the border with MA, not an undervalued mountain hermits paradise.

Every other hill is crowded with bullshit.

Americans don't understand how full up other countries are.

There is literally no where you can go and be more than 15 minutes away from a place selling coca cola and chips.

People bitch about Florida being crowded now, but compared to Japan its Kansas. In Florida there are places you can live where it take you two hours to get to a city, no where in Japan except maybe the forzen wastes of Hokaido where you'll get 50ft of snow in winter and die (out of shape fatass that you are) is like this.

Dido for bongland.

62484807
>>62484699
>Rural Japan is dogshit because its like Southern NH on the border with MA, not an undervalued mountain hermits paradise.
Ball knowledge. Shit like Nashua where it is just a strip mall and it is not nice at all. Exact same vibes in rural Japan for the most part, especially as someone from Boston.

62485115
>>62478389
Mama fricken MIA! are you serious? How often do they come inside. Have you had experience with them?

62485449
I love the cope in this thread. FIrst it's
>oh, cheap house? It's a shitty shack
Then it's
>eww! bugs
Now it's
>I CAN'T GET A 4 SQUARE KILOMETER PIECE OF LAND AND A HOUSE WITH A ROAD THAT IS EXCLUSIVELY FOR ME UNDER $10000! RURAL JAPAN IS GARBAGE!
kek

62485535
>>62484699
You just said buy an affordable shit shack that is 15 minutes away from getting chips and pop. Please tell me where you can do this in America when Blackrock buys up everything?

62485569
>>62478389
I can handle this but the huntsman spiders are a no for me

62485577
>>62478493
Why do some people get so off on demoralisation?

62485613
>>62478389
Can I bread and fry these mofos?
Feed em to my pets?

62485734
>>62477619
These houses are usually built in places where you can no longer build.
So you are literally buying an house you can't fix in the middle of nowhere.

62485869
>>62485535
You can't be poor in Japan and live there.

You can buy a shitty property and watch it decline in value due to the shrinking population, but they aren't going to give you an unlimited visa and let you wage.

>but le why!?

No one is really using Northern Maine.
Regardless of this fact, I don't want Northern Maine being developed and filled with Syrians and Nigerians who could become a problem in 20 years.
But if 1 Nigerian businessman who will be there 8 days a year wants to buy a seaside manor in Kennebunkport and potentially slosh his money around so that we can give all the students at the local school Ipads, thats fine.

62485955
>>62477721
>Renovations are legally required to keep up with modern building codes
Yea, bro. Just keep following jewish building codes. Exponential growth, you always need to progress
>In the middle of nowhere surrounded by boomer japs who are tired of gaijin
Create a business and stop being a freeloader. They'll appreciate it
>A ton of bureacratic hoops to jump through which is very hard if you're not fluent in Japanese or have a girlfriend/wife who is
Rent a friend

62485958
>>62477619
stay in your own ethnostate, timmy. stop coming over here like plague locusts and spreading your explosive diarrhea everywhere

62488023
>>62485958
You have nothing to worry about with most Americans
The would never buy property that is guaranteed to depreciate

62488032
>>62485869
>decline in value due to the shrinking population
That's where you're wrong.
You can buy property in the middle of Tokyo and it will depreciate.
Housing is not a banker's investment scheme for the masses.
It's just a place to live and it deteriorates over time.
The price always goes down in Japan.
That's why 40 year old homes like
>>62481968
>>62482557
are so cheap.
It has nothing to do with bug, or insulation, or Sadako, or anything else.
House price goes down in Japan.
It always goes down.

62488870
>>62477809
>Japan has incredible snow skiing
skiing :-|
skiing in japan :-O

62488884
>>62478562
>and Thailand
Foreigners can't own houses in Thailand because they can't own land. Foreigners can buy a shitty concrete condominium infested with Thai neighbors who will dump garbage all over the property while waiting for someone to build a new condo tower six inches away from their window.

Look up how things went with the View Talay 7 condos. IIRC the foreigner who kept bitching about it was mysteriously shot by a passing motorcyclist for an estimated cost of $200.

62488935
>>62477619
Would be nice if the government left you alone after purchasing it but somehow bureaucracy will make your life hell.

62488951
>>62478539
>you just dont want to put the effort in of being house poor after putting 500k into renovation

62489014
>>62477619
1. These are haunted as fuck
2. These are falling apart
3. These are around absolutely nothing. You will be farming
4. These locals will hate you
